A hands-on walkthrough of access-pattern-first data modeling with the Azure Cosmos DB Agent Kit, GitHub Copilot, and the local emulator
Press enter or click to view image in full size

From One Container Per Table to 75% Fewer RUs: An AI-Guided Cosmos DB RedesignThis is a practical, hands-on look at designing a NoSQL schema with help from an AI coding agent. I walk through a classic e-commerce data model coming off a relational database, show the two modeling mistakes that quietly inflate cost, and then use the Azure Cosmos DB Agent Kit with GitHub Copilot to redesign it around the way the application actually reads and writes data.
By the end, you will know how to document access patterns and volumetrics as context for the agent, how to test designs locally on the emulator before spending a cent in the cloud, and how to measure the difference in request units so the improvement is a solid number.
Reference here.
The problem with NoSQL freedom
NoSQL is easy to start and easy to get wrong. You can evolve a JSON schema without migrations, embed anything inside anything, and reshape documents whenever you like. That flexibility is exactly why so many teams ship designs that look fine against ten test records and then buckle at ten million.
